case_name: OP  v Lancashire Teaching Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust [2021] EWHC 165 (QB) (01 February 2021)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The claimant is aged 15. Acting by his father and litigation friend he brought these clinical negligence proceedings against the NHS Foundation Trust arising from his neonatal management at the Royal Preston Hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He was born by way of emergency caesarean section on the morning of what I will call 'Day 1' in 2005 upon the detection of signs of fetal distress.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Over the course of the night of 'Day 2' to 'Day 3' it is now clear that the claimant was becoming hypoglycaemic as a result of insufficient nutrition.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He was found on the morning of 'Day 3' cold and dangerously ill. Steps to feed him were implemented but were tragically too late to avoid severe brain injury as a result of hypoglycaemia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant's mother had almost no command of English. Allegations in negligence were raised against the Trust for failing to ensure that she understood the requirements of a breastfed newborn.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The defendant NHS Trust denied liability. However, shortly before the liability trial fixed for October 2018 the parties were able to reach a compromise agreement whereby, without admission of liability, the defendant would pay to the claimant damages representing 80% of the value of the claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant has complex needs. He has, accurately, been described as 'grievously injured'. He has 4 limbed cerebral palsy, with global developmental delay, visual impairment and epilepsy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: I am satisfied that the figures and the way in which they are structured and the balance between lump-sum and periodical payments is suitable, fair and appropriate.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I have considered that advice carefully together with the other papers in the case to which I have been referred including a report prepared by an independent financial adviser.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Mr Whitting QC on behalf of the NHS Foundation Trust has today in open court recognised the severity of the claimant's injuries and condition and the challenges that he and his family have faced.
evidence_index.speaker: Defendant's counsel

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Mother had almost no command of English

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Born by emergency caesarean section due to fetal distress. Over the course of the night of 'Day 2' to 'Day 3', the newborn became hypoglycaemic due to insufficient nutrition.

requestables.name: Midwifery notes
requestables.description: Contemporaneous notes regarding breastfeeding and newborn care

reveal.result_summary: Baby put to breast with no apparent problems
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates initial breastfeeding attempt was recorded
reveal.decision_impact: Highlights need for monitoring and education on breastfeeding

timeline_phases.name: Neonatal Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Newborn presents with signs of hypoglycaemia

key_events.event: Emergency caesarean section due to fetal distress
Newborn found cold and dangerously ill

decision_points.prompt: What should have been done to prevent hypoglycaemia?

options.description: Ensure adequate feeding and monitor blood glucose levels
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care to prevent neonatal hypoglycaemia

options.description: Rely on initial breastfeeding attempt without further monitoring
options.clinical_reasoning: Failed to ensure adequate nutrition and monitoring

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant relied on initial breastfeeding attempt

expected_action_court.description: Ensure adequate feeding and monitor blood glucose levels
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence on neonatal care standards

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failed to monitor and ensure adequate nutrition
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Regular monitoring and feeding should have been ensured

patient_outcome.description: Severe brain injury due to hypoglycaemia
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: 4 limbed cerebral palsy, global developmental delay, visual impairment, epilepsy

legal_outcome.damages_awarded: Settlement of £4.5 million lump sum and periodical payments
key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to ensure adequate feeding and monitoring
Causation established - earlier intervention would have prevented injury

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to ensure adequate feeding and monitoring
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Adequate feeding and monitoring would have prevented hypoglycaemia

factual_timeline.event: Emergency caesarean section due to fetal distress
Newborn found cold and dangerously ill

diagnoses.diagnosis: Severe brain injury due to hypoglycaemia

allegations.allegation: Failure to ensure adequate feeding and monitoring
